Details: Various studies assessing the effectiveness of modified and unmodified granular activated carbon (GAC) [2699], as well as other adsorptive media, have reported isotherm data for the removal of arsenic. Modified GAC studies included iron-modified [2697, 2699, 2704, 2707, 2711, 2713], manganese-modified [2707, 2711], copper-modified [2705], calcium-modified [2706], and zirconium-modified [2712] GAC. Other adsorptive media with isotherm data included a macroporous polymer coated with coprecipitated iron–aluminum hydroxides [2700] and multiwall carbon nanotubes with zirconia [2709].
